summary: Crisp, powerful audience recording captures a strong spring‑'77 set highlighted by Sugaree, Scarlet>Fire and a fiery Bertha.
review: The tape is an audience (matrix‑style) capture that many reviewers describe as crisp, full‑range and surprisingly clean for the era. Musically the show shines with a solid first set – opening with a tasty Sugaree and building to a standout Scarlet > Fire – and a second‑set Bertha that ranks among fans’ favorites. Overall the performance is energetic and well‑balanced, making it one of the more underrated spring ’77 shows.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4–5) and enthusiastic comments about both the music and recording quality indicate a strong show with good technical fidelity.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note crisp, full‑range sound with a bit of distortion/reverb but overall clear and very listenable; praised for extra power and presence despite being an audience capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set builds nicely from a strong opening Sugaree, gains momentum through the first‑set climax of Scarlet > Fire, and delivers a memorable second‑set Bertha; a few reviewers mention a rough early start but overall pacing is praised.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solos are described as hot, extra‑sauced and on point throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is highlighted for its range and power, contributing to the force‑of‑nature rhythm section.
band_member_comments.Bob: Keeps solid rhythm guitar work, holding down the groove.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith contributes organ parts; praised overall though one reviewer notes occasional out‑of‑step moments.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey and Bill are lauded as a powerful rhythmic foundation.
